title: Build Sim DB
summary: Build a sim_db Excel workbook from choice-wrangling cookbook output and HB, LC, or LG model betas.
description: sim_db_build converts raw model output into the Excel sim_db format consumed by all sim_run-family commands. It reads a cookbook.json (from choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long) for attribute/level metadata, reads per-respondent (or per-segment) beta draws, and writes a two-sheet workbook: a tables sheet with metadata (attributes, levels, scenarios, xmatrix_logic, subgroups, weights) and a data sheet with respondent betas.

arguments.name: betas_csv
arguments.description: Path to the model output CSV. Format varies by model_type — see model_type for details.

arguments.name: cookbook_json
arguments.description: Path to cookbook.json from choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long. Provides the mapping from estimation column names to human-readable attribute/level labels.

arguments.name: output
arguments.description: Output filename for the generated .xlsx workbook. Relative paths are resolved against --output-dir.

arguments.name: model_type
arguments.description: Type of model that produced betas_csv. One of: hb (model-mnl-hb-mh: has header row, first column is resp_id), hb_stan (model-mnl-hb-stan: no header, no resp_id, columns in cookbook order), lc (model-mnl-lc: coefficient_name × segment matrix), lg (model-mnl-lg: single row of aggregate coefficients).

arguments.name: resp_id_col
arguments.description: Column name for the respondent identifier in betas_csv. Used only for model_type=hb.

arguments.name: subgroup_cols
arguments.description: Column names in betas_csv to include as 0/1 subgroup flag columns in the data sheet. Used only for model_type=hb.

arguments.name: weight_col
arguments.description: Column in betas_csv to use as respondent weight. If absent, all respondents receive equal weight (1.0). Used only for model_type=hb.

arguments.name: scenarios_csv
arguments.description: Path to a scenarios CSV. Required columns: name, product_name, product_label; optional: product_net, product_none, product_availability; then one column per estimation_column from the cookbook. If absent, the scenarios table is empty and a {output}_scenarios_template.csv skeleton is written.

arguments.name: table_sheet
arguments.description: Sheet name for the tables sheet in the output workbook.

arguments.name: data_sheet
arguments.description: Sheet name for the data sheet in the output workbook.

details: The tables sheet uses a horizontal layout: row 0 contains table name markers, row 1 contains column headers, rows 2+ contain data. All sim_run-family commands can read the output directly.
The data sheet uses a vertical layout: row 0 is the header (resp_id, then beta columns, then subgroup columns, then weight). Numeric values are written as Excel numbers for correct arithmetic.

For model_type=hb: betas_csv must have a header row. The resp_id column is identified by resp_id_col. Beta columns are matched against cookbook.estimation_terms by name; extra columns (subgroups, weight) are picked up by subgroup_cols / weight_col.

For model_type=hb_stan: betas_csv has no header row and no resp_id column. Columns must be in the same order as cookbook.estimation_terms. Respondent IDs are assigned sequentially (1, 2, …). Subgroup and weight columns are not supported (part_worths.csv contains only betas).

For model_type=lc: betas_csv is the coefficients.csv from model-mnl-lc, with rows = coefficient names and columns = segments. Each segment becomes one pseudo-respondent. Subgroup membership is not preserved (all segments are assigned equal weight and subgroup flag 1).
For model_type=lg: betas_csv is the coef_table.csv from model-mnl-lg. A single pseudo-respondent row is created from the aggregate estimates. The estimate column is detected automatically by name.
The xmatrix_logic table is auto-populated from the cookbook estimation columns. Logic, interpolation, and cost columns are left blank — fill them in Excel after building if needed.
The attributes table is auto-populated: attributes with level entries become type=discrete; those without become type=continuous.
A {output}_scenarios_template.csv skeleton is always written alongside the .xlsx so users know the exact column format expected for a scenarios_csv input.
value: Automates the most tedious part of the sim workflow — mapping model output into the structured Excel format required by choice-sim. Run once per model, then iterate scenarios freely.
notes: The resulting workbook can be opened in Excel or supplied directly as the excel= input to any sim_run-family command.
After building, fill in the scenarios table (or use the template CSV as a guide) and add the sim_db path to a task.toml to run simulations.
For LC models, consider setting the weight column in the data sheet manually to segment size proportions after building, if you want market-share-weighted averages.

see_also.description: Run a competitive-share simulation using the generated workbook.
Sensitivity sweep driven by the generated workbook.
Grid or zip search using the generated workbook.

examples.title: Build from HB model
examples.body:
[sim_db_build]
betas_csv     = "point_estimates.csv"
cookbook_json = "cookbook.json"
output        = "model.xlsx"
model_type    = "hb"
resp_id_col   = "resp_id"
subgroup_cols = ["total", "male", "female"]
weight_col    = "weight"

examples.title: Build from LC model with scenarios

artifacts.title: Simulator database workbook
artifacts.description: The Excel sim_db workbook consumed by all sim_run-family commands. Contains a 'tables' sheet with product definitions, attribute metadata, and scenarios, and a 'data' sheet with one row per respondent holding their individual preference estimates (part-worths).

fields.name: tables sheet — attributes
fields.description: Product attributes and their levels, defining the simulation space.

fields.name: tables sheet — scenarios
fields.description: Named market scenarios with attribute values per product.

fields.name: data sheet
fields.description: One row per respondent. Columns hold the individual-level preference estimates needed to compute market shares.

artifacts.title: Scenarios template
artifacts.description: A blank CSV skeleton showing the exact column format required to add new scenarios. Fill in product attribute values here and supply the completed file as scenarios_csv on the next build.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Scenario identifier, referenced when running simulations.

fields.name: product_name
fields.description: Internal identifier for the product concept.

fields.name: (attribute columns)
fields.description: One column per attribute from the cookbook, holding the attribute value for this product in this scenario.
